basic_info.headline: AI safety @ Anthropic. 🤖 Global traveler with more than 75 countries of experience. 🌍🌎🌏
basic_info.about:
I put the 'operations' in Tactical Operations, drawing on my strategic mindset, broad engineering background, and extensive global field deployment experience. I thrive on bridging the gap between operational needs and engineering requirements, and speak fluently in both languages. I also enjoy leading and empowering teams of colleagues and volunteers to identify client pain points and produce impactful solutions. At the same time, I love building and tinkering, so I'm never too far away from the code & console. #OpsGeek 

I am incredibly passionate about the common mission among several organizations I have worked and volunteered with - bringing ubiquitous communications to the world, especially in disasters.

I am always looking to maximize my impact, and welcome others looking to do the same to get in touch. (I get quite a bit of outreach these days, so apologies in advance if I'm unable to get back to everyone.)

Please do not contact me if you are with a recruiting agency seeking to place candidates at Anthropic, or if you are with an expert network looking to have me do a compensated expertise request or similar that describes Anthropic's research or operations.

experience.title: Member of Technical Staff & Director of Bubble Tea Center of Excellence
Special Programs Director
experience.description:
I led projectConnect, a program dedicated to helping to bridge the digital divide, exacerbated by COVID-19, by installing community WiFi access points. A "full-stack" leader, I define the program vision, develop relationships with community partners and donors, rally volunteers and field teams, create software infrastructure to support installation of more than a thousand sites, engineer networks for unusual requirements, and much more. I absolutely love what we've been able to accomplish as an organization through this program, having heard incredible stories of the impact we've made simply by expanding access to the internet during the heights of the pandemic.

I am also particularly proud of the response we developed for the 2021 Haiti Earthquake, leading multiple waves of volunteers to establish communications for medical field teams, food and shelter distribution, and early childhood protection.

experience.title: Region 9 Director
experience.description: I led other volunteers in disaster deployments & operations with a focus in Region 9 (AZ, CA, NV, HI & Pacific Islands). I also developed and led technical and operational training sessions to prepare volunteers for deployment with ITDRC. To support our disaster responses, I established and maintained relationships with other technology providers, government agencies, and non-profits.

experience.title: Volunteer
experience.description: I deployed to disasters including hurricanes, tornadoes, and wildfires - supporting disaster relief by providing technical assistance in scoping, engineering, installing, maintaining, and recovering IP networks and other IT assets.

experience.title: Telecommunications Specialist
experience.company: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS)
experience.description:
National Disaster Medical System (NDMS) - Logistics Response Assistance Team (LRAT) - Disaster Medical Assistance Team (DMAT)

Connecting wires - and people - helping to deliver medical care in disasters.

experience.title: Tactical Operations Coordinator
experience.description:
I served as an operations coordinator for Cisco Tactical Operations (TacOps), a team with a pro-bono mission to deliver emergency communications infrastructure to disasters around the globe. I spearheaded software development efforts and was responsible for driving innovative development projects in addition to coordinating team deployments, including to Antigua and Barbuda (Hurricane Irma), Northern California wildfires, and Guatemala (Volcan de Fuego). I also managed our crisis connectivity consulting program and led efforts to engage with private, public, and non-profit partners critical to effective disaster response.

Cisco Tactical Operations (TacOps) is a pro-bono disaster response group that provides emergency communications infrastructure in disasters like earthquakes, typhoons, and wildfires. We work together with government agencies, non-profits, and international organizations to establish data, voice, and radio capabilities, providing connectivity to first responders, governments, critical infrastructure, and affected populations.

https://www.cisco.com/go/tacops

featured.title: Careers
featured.description:
Anthropic is coming to BSidesSF this weekend! Jackie Bow, Peter Sanford, Ziyad Edher, Chris Norman, and Doug Wilson will be presenting at the conference: https://lnkd.in/d9zbZ8yZ 

Many more Anthropic staff will at the conference; we'll be on the lookout for people to join our awesome teams. We have roles open in Security, Software Engineering, Safeguards (Trust & Safety), Research, Frontier Red Team, and more: https://lnkd.in/dy2iPg_E

featured.title: Anthropic Mixer at BSidesSF & RSA · Luma
featured.description:
We're having a pre-BSidesSF/RSA madness happy hour in San Francisco on Friday 4/25! RSVP here: https://lnkd.in/gBVJPiaG 

Come chat with our security team about what we're doing as a key part of our AI safety and Responsible Scaling Policy agendas. We're also looking for great security minds to join our team - we've got a lot of exciting projects brewing!

Good demo of Claude-powered coding similar to the workflow I use! 
https://lnkd.in/d94qTrYW

Also... we're hiring!

featured.title: Claude 3.5 Sonnet for sparking creativity
featured.description: Check out this demo of our newest model! We've been using this model internally, and the speed and quality of its responses are impressive. Try it for Claw'd. 🦀

What a ride - we launched Claude 3 last month which feels so great to use on intelligence (Opus), speed (Haiku), and balance (Sonnet), and launched tool use in beta yesterday! If you want to come join work with incredibly smart and kind colleagues on interesting projects likely to make a high impact, we have 62 roles open across the company (that's more than the number of people when I joined!). I'd especially like to highlight some roles on our security teams:

- Application Security Engineer: https://lnkd.in/gAfsJbvf
- Senior Software Security Engineer: https://lnkd.in/g4AaZESG
- Principal Software Security Engineer: https://lnkd.in/g8PZRv4e
- Detection and Response Engineer: https://lnkd.in/gWUKGcyR
- IT Engineer: https://lnkd.in/gv7nWRTE

Have I mentioned the snacks? 🧋
